title: Centaurea eflanensis
taxonomy: Centaurea eflanensis is classified under the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Centaurea, a group of thistle-like flowering plants. Centaurea eflanensis is further classified as a species of thistle. The plant is native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe and is often found in dry, rocky areas. Its classification is used to distinguish it from other related plant species within the Centaurea genus.

intro: Centaurea eflanensis, also known as Eflan's knapweed, is a perennial plant in the Asteraceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Centaurea eflanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
